# `simulation.ron` ŌĆö field reference

<!-- GENERATED by `cargo run -p dc_data_docs` (dronecom #2815). Do not edit: field docs live as /// comments on the Rust structs. -->

Data: [`config/simulation.ron`](../config/simulation.ron)

## `SimConfig`

Consolidated simulation configuration ŌĆö all sim tuning parameters in a
single resource, loaded from `assets/config/simulation.ron`.

### `autopilot` : [`AutopilotSection`](#autopilotsection)

Navigation and flight-execution tuning: waypoint-capture thresholds,
per-domain terrain clearance, climb/descent rates, formation reforming,
and terminal homing. Consumed by the autopilot executor (`dc_autopilot`).

### `launch` : [`LaunchSection`](#launchsection)

Launch and recovery pipeline tuning: deck-launch clearance and drop, loiter
altitudes, the fixed-wing holding pattern, and well-deck / flight-deck
landing geometry. Consumed by the deck-ops handler (`dc_sim`) and the
recovery autopilot (`dc_autopilot`).

### `debug_spawn` : [`DebugSpawnSection`](#debugspawnsection)

Placement of debug-spawned entities: default aircraft altitude, submarine
depth, and terrain clearances. Consumed by the `DebugCommand::DebugSpawn`
handler.

### `sensors` : [`SensorSection`](#sensorsection)

Sensor-pipeline tuning: contact retention, the recursive track filter,
bearing-only and TMA velocity solvers, formation clustering, contact
classification, and fire-control locks. Consumed by the sensor systems
(`dc_sensors`).

### `sectors` : [`SectorSection`](#sectorsection)

Team sector geometry ŌĆö the arcs on the circular map border where each
team's carriers start (`dc_scenario::sectors`). Consumed by scenario setup
when placing carriers.

### `intercept` : [`InterceptSection`](#interceptsection)

Intercept / standoff behaviour tuning: the standoff-range feedback
controller, the last-known-position search spiral, the battle-damage
assessment orbit, and climb-to-target. Consumed by the intercept tasking
and its autopilot dispatch (`dc_tasking` / `dc_autopilot`).

### `patrol` : [`PatrolSection`](#patrolsection)

Patrol boundary tuning: the minimum orbit radius and the map-edge buffer a
patrol must fit inside. Consumed by patrol tasking and patrol placement.

### `servicing` : [`ServicingSection`](#servicingsection)

Servicing tuning: fuel reserves and auto-RTB margins, refuel / rearm
transfer rates, and purchase build-time scaling. Consumed by the servicing
and deck-ops systems (`dc_sim`).

### `acoustic` : [`AcousticConfig`](#acousticconfig)

Acoustic-propagation tuning for sonar detection: thermocline loss, shallow-
water attenuation, and deep-water convergence zones. Consumed by
passive/active sonar detection.

### `match_end` : [`MatchEndSection`](#matchendsection)

Imminent match-end warning tuning ŌĆö the seconds-remaining thresholds at
which the host broadcasts a `MatchEndingSoon` warning. Consumed by the host
match timer.

## `AutopilotSection`

Autopilot tuning parameters.

### `waypoint_threshold` : f32

Waypoint reached when within this distance (metres).

### `sea_terrain_clearance` : f32

Additional clearance below draft for surface ship navigation (metres).

### `air_terrain_clearance` : f32

Clearance above terrain for fixed-wing aircraft waypoints (metres).

### `submarine_terrain_clearance` : f32

Clearance above seabed for submarine navigation (metres). The keelŌåÆseabed
gap kept by routing, pathfinding, and reactive avoidance (#1983).

### `submarine_surface_clearance` : f32

No-broach margin (metres): a submerged submarine keeps its masthead at
least this far below the sea surface. The submerged no-broach ceiling is
`-(sail_height + this)` (#1983).

### `edge_projection_min_size` : f32

Minimum node edge length (`half_size * 2`) for edge-projected waypoints.
Nodes smaller than this use their center. Default 400m.

### `aircraft_descent_rate_multiplier` : f32

Fixed-wing descent rate as a multiple of `climb_rate`.
DC-74: replace with proper dive physics (total speed = `max_speed` split
between horizontal and vertical components).

### `submarine_max_depth_change_m` : f32

Maximum depth change (metres) a submarine route may use to clear a
feature before it prefers to route *around* it at depth. The en-route
ceiling is capped at `from_y + this`; a feature needing more ascent
becomes non-navigable so the A* gate routes around. A full-envelope
fallback still reaches targets that require a larger ascent. #1865.

### `air_climb_rate_fraction` : f32

Fraction (0,1] of an aircraft's max climb rate used for en-route
terrain planning. A gentler routing gradient makes steep/close peaks
non-navigable so the gate routes around them; gentle/distant climbs
still go over. Falls back to the full rate if no route is found. #1865.

### `terrain_avoidance` : [`TerrainAvoidanceConfig`](#terrainavoidanceconfig)

Terrain avoidance tuning (look-ahead distance, avoidance angles).
Shared across all vehicle autopilots.

### `group_target_lookahead_secs` : f32

Forward-lookahead horizon (seconds) applied when computing the
leader's autopilot group target position ŌĆö
`target_xz = pos_xz + heading_unit * speed * lookahead_secs`.
Consumed by formation followers to station-keep against a
position slightly ahead of the leader rather than at the leader
itself, reducing position lag at higher speeds. DC-797.

### `formation_reform_gain` : f32

Proportional gain (per second) for a formation follower closing the
distance to its slot. The follower is commanded the velocity vector
`leader_velocity + slot_error_vec * formation_reform_gain` ŌĆö it steers
along that vector at its magnitude, capped at its own max speed. At the
slot the command equals the leader's velocity; a follower *behind* its
slot is driven faster to close, so a member displaced for an engagement
reforms even behind a stationary (idle/holding) leader where matching the
leader's zero speed would strand it (DC-1022 M4), while one *ahead* of its
slot is commanded below the leader's speed so it falls back rather than
overshooting into a limit cycle (DC-1025).

### `formation_reform_enter_m` : f32

Slot error (metres) at which a Multi-group leader *starts* reserving
catch-up headroom below its speed envelope. The upper edge of the
hysteresis band; must be > `formation_reform_exit_m`. DC-1021.

### `formation_reform_exit_m` : f32

Slot error (metres) below which a leader stops reserving headroom and
ramps back up toward the envelope max ŌĆö every member is "in slot". The
lower edge of the hysteresis band; must be < `formation_reform_enter_m`.
Between the two thresholds the headroom holds while a lagging member is
still closing and releases once it is steady at its geometric floor
(see `formation_reform_converge_min_mps`). DC-1021 / DC-1155.

### `formation_catchup_headroom_frac` : f32

Fraction of the group `SpeedEnvelope.max` a leader gives up as catch-up
headroom while fully reforming. At headroom factor 1.0 the leader is
capped at `envelope.max * (1 - frac)` (floored at `envelope.min`), so the
slowest member ŌĆö whose own max equals `envelope.max` ŌĆö has a real speed
delta to close its slot. DC-1021.

### `formation_headroom_ramp_per_sec` : f32

Rate (per second) at which the leader's headroom factor ramps between 0
(full speed) and 1 (full headroom). Smooths the commanded-speed change so
the leader eases off and ramps back up rather than stepping. DC-1021.

### `formation_reform_converge_min_mps` : f32

Minimum closing rate (m/s) for a member inside the reform hysteresis
dead-band to count as "still catching up", which keeps the leader's
headroom engaged. Below this the member is treated as steady at its
geometric (precession) floor and the leader releases headroom ŌĆö so a
well-formed orbiting group stops chronically shaving patrol speed, while a
genuinely lagging member on a straight leg (closing at a real speed delta)
still holds the leader back until it is in slot. DC-1155.

### `terminal_homing` : [`TerminalHomingSection`](#terminalhomingsection)

Terminal-homing substep tuning (DC-976).

### `patrol_loop` : [`PatrolLoopSection`](#patrolloopsection)

Route-across patrol-loop ring-sampling tuning (#1771 phase 2).

### `lead_velocity_noise_tolerance_rad` : f32

Max tolerable lead-pursuit heading wobble (radians) from noise in the
estimated target velocity (#2352). A lead-pursuit attacker aims at
`contact_pos + contact_vel┬Ęt`; a per-scan velocity error `Žā_v` swings the
commanded heading by `Ōēł Žā_v / V_closing`. The lead velocity is scaled by
`min(1, this ┬Ę weapon_speed / Žā_v)` (see `dc_physics::guidance::lead_confidence`)
so that jitter is bounded to this value. `Žā_v = 0` (exact sensing) ŌćÆ full,
unchanged lead ŌĆö Clean-sensing guidance is untouched. Self-scales across
weapon speeds: a slow torpedo damps hard, a fast missile barely at all.

### `munition_battery_scuttle_secs` : f32

How long (seconds) a spent munition (`VehicleKind::Projectile`) that has
run its fuel dry and switched to battery power may persist before it
**scuttles** (#2520). Once fuel is out a projectile can no longer power
toward its target ŌĆö a missile coasts on residual kinetic energy, a
torpedo coasts to a stop ŌĆö so it is spent; without this bound an
underwater round (no fixed/rotary-wing terrain-collision despawn) sits
dead-in-the-water indefinitely, pinging its seeker on hotel battery
(~1 h on a 1 kWh / 1 kW MK-55) as an immortal navigation hazard / decoy.
At this age-on-battery the round scuttles as a spent one
(`LostReason::MunitionSpent` ŌåÆ LOGISTICS; a *platform* that runs its
battery flat is `BatteryDepleted` instead, #2560). Sized well above any
legitimate battery-phase terminal coast so a real intercept is never cut
short. Platforms are unaffected ŌĆö only projectiles scuttle on this bound.

### `munition_out_of_bounds_scuttle_margin_m` : f32

How far (metres) beyond the quadtree extent (`half_extent`) a projectile
may stray before it is scuttled (#2515). `half_extent` ŌĆö `radius` rounded
up to a power-of-2 cell multiple ŌĆö is the exact boundary past which
`find_path`'s start gate returns `None`: a round out there can never route
back and mills as an immortal out-of-bounds zombie (playtest tracks at
59ŌĆō67 km on a 50 km map, whose `half_extent` is 51.2 km). Keyed on
`half_extent` (which scales with map size) rather than `radius`, so a
legitimate engagement operating in the `radius..half_extent` ring ŌĆö e.g. a
launcher standing off from a near-rim target ŌĆö keeps the headroom it needs;
only genuinely unroutable strays past `half_extent + margin` are cut.
Scuttled as `LostReason::StrayedOutOfBounds` ŌåÆ LOGISTICS. Only projectiles
are bounded; platforms are recalled by their commander, not scuttled.

## `LaunchSection`

Tuning parameters for the launch pipeline, loaded from
`assets/config/simulation.ron` (launch section).

### `launch_altitude_offset` : f32

Height (metres) of a carrier's flight deck above the carrier's own
reference point. Added to the carrier position to get the deck altitude a
launching or landing aircraft targets.

### `launch_clearance_distance` : f32

Distance (metres) a freshly-launched aircraft is sent clear of the carrier
before its mission begins ŌĆö the length of the initial departure leg issued
by the deck-ops launch handler.

### `well_deck_reverse_speed` : f32

Magnitude (m/s) of the initial launch impulse for sea-surface and
submarine hulls: forward along the launcher heading for a tube launch,
astern (negated) when backing out of the well deck on a reverse-direction
launcher. Applied once at spawn by the deck-ops launch handler.

### `default_loiter_altitude_fixed_wing` : f32

Default loiter altitude (metres above sea level) for a launched fixed-wing
drone that has no commanded altitude yet.

### `default_loiter_altitude_rotary_wing` : f32

Default loiter altitude (metres above sea level) for a launched
rotary-wing drone that has no commanded altitude yet.

### `air_launch_drop_distance` : f32

Vertical drop (metres) below the launching aircraft at which an
air-launched munition or vehicle is released.

### `holding_pattern_length` : f32

Straight length (metres) of the fixed-wing recovery holding pattern ŌĆö the
distance between the two semicircular ends of the racetrack.

### `holding_pattern_width` : f32

Width (metres) of the fixed-wing recovery holding pattern ŌĆö the separation
between the two straight legs of the racetrack. Sized so the semicircular
ends fit the widest fixed-wing turn radius (#1673).

### `holding_pattern_altitude` : f32

Altitude (metres above sea level) flown while waiting in the fixed-wing
recovery holding pattern.

### `holding_pattern_entry_threshold` : f32

Distance (metres) within which a drone is treated as having reached the
holding-pattern entry point and begins flying the racetrack.

### `holding_pattern_turn_radius` : f32

Corner-rounding turn radius (metres) for the holding-pattern racetrack,
kept equal to half the pattern width (the semicircle radius of the ends).

### `holding_pattern_speed_multiplier` : f32

Multiplier on the carrier's speed that sets the drone's holding-pattern
airspeed, so it stays ahead of the moving carrier. Clamped between the
drone's stall speed and the fastest speed its turn radius can fly the
pattern.

### `landing_arrival_threshold` : f32

Altitude margin (metres) above the deck at which a descending fixed-wing is
considered to have arrived over the deck for landing.

### `landing_final_approach_lookahead` : f32

Convergence distance (metres) for the fixed-wing final-approach
cross-track controller. During `Landing::Descending` the drone tracks
the straight line through the carrier along its deck heading; the
commanded intercept angle is `atan(cross_error / this)`, clamped to a
maximum, so the drone closes large lateral errors at a decisive angle
and tracks the centreline tightly once near it ŌĆö flying *through* the
deck instead of orbiting the carrier point (which a finite turn radius
makes impossible).

### `landing_capture_cross_half_width` : f32

Cross-track half-width (metres) of the deck-aligned landing capture
zone, measured perpendicular to the deck heading. The drone is captured
only when lined up with the deck within this lateral tolerance.

### `landing_capture_along_half_length` : f32

Along-track half-length (metres) of the deck-aligned landing capture
zone, measured along the deck heading from the carrier point. Generous
enough that a drone flying the final approach at speed cannot skip over
the zone between ticks.

### `well_deck_recovery_follow_distance` : f32

Distance (metres) astern of the carrier a well-deck-recovering craft holds
while it waits for a free deck slot.

### `well_deck_arrival_threshold` : f32

Distance (metres) from the carrier within which a well-deck-recovering
craft is considered to have arrived and is taken aboard; the craft also
begins decelerating four times this distance out.

### `well_deck_holding_station_spacing_m` : f32

Spacing (metres) between successive well-deck holding stations, strung
astern of the carrier along its heading (#2627). `recovery_enqueue`
admits every weight-eligible candidate into `WellDeckApproach::Approaching`
on the same tick, decoupled from the single serial "approach" phase slot ŌĆö
without a per-rank offset, every admitted hull steers straight at
`carrier_pos` and stacks on the same coordinates. The hull occupying the
physical approach slot still rendezvouses at `carrier_pos` (rank 0);
each hull still waiting in the pipeline queue holds `rank * this` metres
astern, rank being its live position in that queue.

### `rally_radius_m` : f32

Radius (metres) within which all group members must arrive before the
group transitions out of `TaskingKind::FormingUp`. Used by the
`FormingUp` tick body (Task 16).

## `DebugSpawnSection`

Configuration for debug spawn placement, loaded from
`assets/config/simulation.ron` (`debug_spawn` section).

### `air_default_altitude` : f32

Default altitude for spawned aircraft (metres).

### `air_terrain_clearance` : f32

Clearance above terrain for aircraft when terrain exceeds default altitude.

### `sub_default_depth` : f32

Default depth for spawned submarines (negative, metres).

### `sub_terrain_clearance` : f32

Minimum clearance above seabed for submarines.

## `SensorSection`

Tuning parameters for the sensor pipeline, loaded from
`assets/config/simulation.ron` (sensors section).

### `stale_retention` : [`StaleRetentionSection`](#staleretentionsection)

Retention policy for `Lost` contacts before they're purged from the
commander's contact picture (#2375).

### `contact_capacity_hysteresis` : f32

Incumbent-track hysteresis for capped sensors (DC-504): a held track's
effective ranking distance is scaled by `(1 - this)` so it is not
displaced by an only-marginally-closer newcomer. Range 0.0..1.0.

### `filter` : [`TrackFilterSection`](#trackfiltersection)

Recursive track-filter tuning (DC-36.1). Used only when a scenario runs
with noisy sensing enabled.

### `noise` : [`MeasurementNoiseSection`](#measurementnoisesection)

Measurement-noise model tuning (DC-36.1): how the authored per-sensor
sigmas scale with SNR. Used only when noisy sensing is enabled.

### `bearing` : [`BearingOnlySection`](#bearingonlysection)

Bearing-only passive sensor tuning (DC-36.2). Used only when noisy
sensing is enabled.

### `tma` : [`TmaSection`](#tmasection)

Batch pseudo-linear TMA velocity solver tuning (DC-1053). Used only when
noisy sensing is enabled.

### `clustering` : [`ClusteringSection`](#clusteringsection)

Cluster-reconciliation tuning (DC-36.3): formation merging. Used only when
noisy sensing is enabled.

### `classification` : [`ClassificationSection`](#classificationsection)

Contact-classification tuning (DC-36.5): the staged chassis classifier and
its NTDS-tree projection. Used only when noisy sensing is enabled.

### `fire_control` : [`FireControlSection`](#firecontrolsection)

Fire-control lock tuning (DC-946.1): acquisition dwell and lock-drop
timeout for the cued-lock pass in `run_detection`.

## `SectorSection`

Configuration for team sector geometry. Loaded from
`assets/config/simulation.ron` (sectors section).

### `access_fraction` : f32

Fraction of each team's full segment that forms the access arc.

### `min_carrier_separation_deg` : f32

Minimum angular separation between carriers of the same team (degrees).

## `InterceptSection`

Intercept behavior tuning parameters. Loaded from
`assets/config/simulation.ron` (intercept section).

### `fallback_standoff_range` : f32

Fallback standoff distance (metres) used when the dynamic feedback
controller has no SNR data yet (e.g. first frame of intercept).

### `min_standoff_range` : f32

Minimum standoff range floor (metres). The feedback controller will
never command a range below this value.

### `max_standoff_range` : f32

Maximum standoff range ceiling (metres). The feedback controller will
never command a range above this value.

### `standoff_reach_margin` : f32

Safety margin (fraction in `(0, 1]`) applied to the *weapon's* static
`max_range` when it caps the standoff orbit ceiling. The standoff orbit
radius is governed by the longest stowed weapon's static `max_range`
(`crate::StandoffWeapon::Selected`), but the launch gate fires on the
*kinematic* reach `reach_m` (`Vehicle::classify_reach`), which is `<=`
the static max and shrinks as the launcher decelerates to orbit. Without
this margin a strong SNR pushes the orbit out to the static ceiling, which
sits a few metres *outside* the kinematic firing envelope ŌĆö the launcher
parks just beyond its own reach and never fires (#2212 deadlock). The
margin pulls the orbit inside the envelope so the gate can release. It
applies only when the weapon range is the binding cap, never to
`max_standoff_range`.

### `standoff_adjust_rate` : f32

Rate at which the standoff range adjusts (metres per second).

### `dead_band_db` : f32

SNR dead band in dB. The feedback controller does not adjust range
when SNR is within this margin of the doctrine target.

### `search_radial_speed_fraction` : f32

Outward (radial) speed of the LKP search spiral, as a fraction of the
drone's commanded forward speed. The spiral radius grows by
`fraction ├Ś forward_speed` metres per second, so a faster drone (likely
chasing a faster target that drifted further) searches a larger area in
the same time. Keep below ~0.3 so the spiral stays tight enough to cover.

### `search_duration_seconds` : f32

Seconds the drone spends flying the expanding LKP search spiral before
giving up (`SearchingLKP` ŌåÆ `Complete`). The timer runs only once the
drone has reached the datum (the spiral phase), not during transit.

### `search_spiral_start_radius_m` : f32

Distance (metres) within which the drone is considered to have reached
the dead-reckoned datum ŌĆö it then switches from transit to the expanding
spiral, which begins at this radius.

### `bda_orbit_radius` : f32

Orbit radius (metres) held while awaiting battle-damage assessment
after a weapon detonates (`InterceptPhase::AwaitingBda`).

### `climb_to_target_min_delta_m` : f32

#2604: vertical delta (metres) above which an engaging shooter climbs
toward a target that is above it. When a commanded (Engage) target is
more than this far above the launcher, tasking raises the shooter's
commanded altitude toward the target (raise-only, capped at the launcher's
ceiling) so it closes the vertical geometry and the slant range shrinks
into the weapon's reach envelope. Below this delta the vertical barely
degrades the slant, so no climb is commanded.

### `interrogation_max_chase_speed_mps` : f32

#2685: horizontal speed (m/s) at or below which a closing Unknown contact
is worth *interrogating* by closing range. A screen holding an Unknown
closer only peels off to close-to-identify when the contact is this slow
or slower ŌĆö a faster inbound (e.g. a ~600 m/s missile) it cannot
meaningfully close on, so it holds station and engages the instant IFF
resolves via the standing defend-anchor scan (no futile chase). The
interrogation *leash* is not a separate constant ŌĆö it is tied to the
engagement-scan geometry (`CPA_LOOKAHEAD_MULT ├Ś engagement_range.inner`) so
a legitimately-admitted contact never trips it.

## `PatrolSection`

Patrol boundary tuning parameters. Loaded from
`assets/config/simulation.ron` (patrol section).

### `min_radius` : f32

Minimum patrol orbit radius in metres.

### `boundary_buffer` : f32

Inset from map boundary in metres ŌĆö patrols must fit within
`radius - boundary_buffer`.

## `ServicingSection`

Servicing tuning parameters: fuel, refueling, and rearming transfer rates.

### `auto_rtb_safety_margin` : f32

Multiplier applied to estimated fuel-to-return distance.
Higher values trigger RTB earlier. 1.0 = no margin.

### `low_fuel_check_threshold` : f32

Fuel fraction below which the carrier distance check runs each tick.
Above this threshold, fuel is assumed sufficient and the check is skipped.

### `bingo_reserve_fuel_fraction` : f32

Absolute fuel-reserve floor (fraction) that triggers bingo/RTB
independently of distance-to-carrier (#2453). The distance-based
"point of no return" test collapses to ~0 for a unit stationed on its
carrier (a screen escort), so it never fires; this floor makes such a
unit come home once its fuel drops to the reserve rather than burning
dry on station. OR'd with the distance test, so distant units ŌĆö which
hit the distance term at higher fuel ŌĆö keep their existing bingo timing.

### `recovery_pattern_overhead_m` : f32

Fuel-range reserve (metres) added to the distance-to-recovery-deck before
the safety-margin scale, so the distance-based bingo budgets for the
recovery pattern every unit flies on arrival ŌĆö holding-pattern entry,
racetrack laps, and the landing/well-deck approach ŌĆö not just the
straight-line transit to the carrier's centre (#2519). Sized from the
fixed-wing holding-pattern geometry (`launch.holding_pattern_*`): roughly
one racetrack lap (`2┬Ęlength + pi┬Ęwidth ~= 5 km` for the default 1500x650
pattern) plus the landing approach. Without it a far-stationed small-tank
unit only ever bingos on the geometry-blind `bingo_reserve_fuel_fraction`
floor ŌĆö too late to complete transit + pattern + margin ŌĆö and ditches in
or short of the pattern. A single fixed-wing-derived reserve is applied to
every domain (rotary hover-descent, surface/submarine well-deck approach);
that is generous for the shorter well-deck approach but conservative in
the safe direction (a ditch is the reported failure).

### `rtb_divert_hysteresis_kg` : f32

Extra headroom (kg) beyond the drone's own weight that an *alternative*
carrier must have before a holding autonomous drone diverts to it. The
hysteresis margin prevents diverting to a deck that barely fits and is
likely to fill before arrival. See DC-35.8.

### `refuel_rate_kg_per_second` : f32

Fuel transfer rate from carrier to each refueling drone (kg/s).
Each drone gets the full rate simultaneously.

### `fuel_order_quantity_kg` : f32

Weight of fuel per fuel order (kg).

### `fuel_order_cost` : f32

Cost per fuel order (dollars).

### `transfer_rate_kg_per_second` : f32

Transfer rate for rearming (kg/s). Each transfer progresses at this rate.

### `cost_to_seconds_rate` : f32

Build time in simulated seconds per unit of vehicle cost. The base
build time of a purchase is `cost * cost_to_seconds_rate`, then scaled
by the per-game `build_time_multiplier` (seconds per unit of cost).

## `AcousticConfig`

Acoustic propagation tuning parameters for sonar detection.

### `critical_angle_deg` : f32

Critical angle (degrees) below which sound cannot cross the thermocline.

### `max_layer_loss_db` : f32

Maximum thermocline attenuation at shallow grazing angles (dB).

### `min_layer_loss_db` : f32

Residual thermocline loss even at steep angles (dB).

### `shallow_water_k` : f32

Shallow water attenuation rate constant (dB/km).

### `shallow_water_p` : f32

Shallow water depth exponent ŌĆö controls steepness at shallow end.

### `shallow_water_max_depth` : f32

Depth beyond which shallow water loss is zero (metres).

### `cz_ref_depth` : f32

Reference depth for full deep-water convergence zone (metres).

### `cz_ref_interval_nm` : f32

CZ ring interval at reference depth (nautical miles).

### `cz_ref_gain_db` : f32

Peak gain of first CZ ring at reference depth (dB).

### `cz_width_ratio` : f32

Ring width as a fraction of interval.

### `cz_falloff_per_ring_db` : f32

Gain reduction per successive ring (dB).

### `cz_q` : f32

Depth scaling exponent for CZ gain.

### `cz_min_depth` : f32

Minimum water depth required for convergence zone formation (metres).

## `MatchEndSection`

Imminent match-end warning tuning, loaded from
`assets/config/simulation.ron` (`match_end` section).

### `warning_thresholds_secs` : `Vec`<u32>

Imminent-end warning thresholds, in seconds remaining, strictly
descending (e.g. `[300, 60]` for T-5min and T-1min). The host emits a
`MatchEndingSoon` broadcast as the match timer crosses each threshold.
May be empty to disable warnings entirely.

## `TerrainAvoidanceConfig`

Tuning for terrain-aware climbing/steering in autopilots.

### `look_ahead_seconds` : f32

How many seconds ahead to look for terrain (distance = speed * this value).

### `avoidance_angles` : `Vec`<f32>

Escalating angles (degrees) to try when ahead is blocked.

## `TerminalHomingSection`

Terminal-homing substep tuning (DC-976). When a warhead-bearing
fixed-wing attacker is within `gate_range_steps` frame-steps of its
target, the autopilot hands physics a homing directive and physics
integrates guidance+flight in substeps of at most `max_substep_secs`.

The `off_nose_*` / `turn_speed_frac` fields tune terminal energy
management: turn radius grows with speed (r = v/Žē at the yaw-rate
clamp), so when the target sits far off the missile's nose the homing
loop commands a lower speed ŌĆö drag bleeds energy and the turn
tightens ŌĆö then re-accelerates once nose-on.

### `gate_range_steps` : f32

Engage when `range < max(speed * dt * gate_range_steps,
max_speed * terminal_gate_secs)`.

### `terminal_gate_secs` : f32

dt-independent terminal-endgame time-to-go (s) at the mover's **max
speed**, a lower bound on the terminal range gate shared by both the
homing-entry gate and the #2290 one-shot spend gate: `terminal_range =
max(speed┬Ędt┬Ęgate_range_steps, max_speed┬Ęterminal_gate_secs)` (#2373).
`speed┬Ędt┬Ęgate_range_steps` (current speed) collapses to ~the fuze radius
when a lofted missile coasts down to a low terminal speed, so the missile
enters substepped homing too late to null the dive lead error AND the
spend gate has no margin outside the fuze ŌĆö a pass just outside the fuze
then neither fuzes nor spends and loops to battery death. Keying the floor
to `max_speed` (a capability, not the coasted speed) makes it
dt-independent AND scales it with the mover: a fast one-shot missile gets a
terminal envelope larger than its miss (M-250 ~266 m), while a slow
re-attacking torpedo gets a floor *below its fuze radius* (~6ŌĆō8 m) so its
spend gate stays subsumed by the fuze and its behaviour is unchanged.
`max(...)` never narrows the gate below `speed┬Ędt┬Ęgate_range_steps`, so
coarse-dt behaviour is unchanged.

### `max_substep_secs` : f32

Maximum integration substep (seconds) while homing.

### `off_nose_full_speed_rad` : f32

Off-nose angle (radians) at or below which full intercept speed is
commanded.

### `off_nose_min_speed_rad` : f32

Off-nose angle (radians) at or beyond which the commanded speed
bottoms out at `intercept_speed * turn_speed_frac`.

### `turn_speed_frac` : f32

Fraction of intercept speed commanded when fully off-nose. Floored
at the airframe's `min_speed` (never command below stall).

### `los_lead_secs` : f32

Lead time (seconds) for the **bearing-only** lead-pursuit aim (#2197).
The angle-domain law aims a fixed lead time ahead of the line of sight
(`los_dir + los_lead_secs ┬Ę los_rate`) ŌĆö a dt-independent aim *point*, so
the integrator turns at full authority at every tick / substep (a
dt-scaled aim only nulled the crossing miss via the #1835 coarse-step
over-turn artifact). Larger leads pull harder onto a fast-rotating LOS.

## `PatrolLoopSection`

Ring-sampling tuning for the route-across patrol loop (#1771 phase 2).

When an `Order::Orbit` ring crosses impassable terrain, the ring is sampled
into points classified navigable/blocked, and the navigable arcs are joined
by bridge paths into a closed loop. Sample count is adaptive to the ring
circumference: `(circumference / sample_spacing_m)` clamped to
`[min_samples, max_samples]`. Spacing is roughly one sample per terrain-cell
width ŌĆö fine enough to catch a short navigable nub, coarse enough not to
waste samples. (Density only affects arc/gap classification; bridge A* runs
on the full-resolution quadtree, so thin straits are still found.)

### `sample_spacing_m` : f32

Target arc length (metres) between adjacent ring samples.

### `min_samples` : u32

Minimum ring samples regardless of circumference (small rings).

### `max_samples` : u32

Maximum ring samples regardless of circumference (large rings).

### `follow_lookahead_m` : f32

Corner-rounding lookahead (metres) for the loop follower. Kept on the
order of the sample spacing ŌĆö the per-domain *routing* lookahead is tuned
for long legs and would cut a dense loop's corners across the blocked
arcs the loop exists to avoid.

### `follow_entry_threshold_m` : f32

Distance (metres) at which the loop follower advances to the next loop
vertex (proximity advance; the passed-corner test also advances).

### `bridge_reversal_threshold_deg` : f32

Turn angle (degrees) at an arcŌåÆbridge junction above which the shortest
bridge is treated as an unflyable reversal ("hairpin") and re-planned the
sweep-continuous way around the obstacle (#1801). The shortest A* route
around a large obstacle can sit on the same side as the navigable arc,
producing a hairpin tip no airframe can fly; when the bridge departs its
arc-end more than this angle off the patrol sweep tangent, it is rebuilt
around the far (sweep-continuous) side instead.

### `bridge_clearance_margin_m` : f32

Lateral clearance (metres) kept between the patrol loop and impassable
terrain (#1801). The re-planned sweep-continuous bridge is anchored this
far beyond the navigability boundary so the followed path ŌĆö with the
airframe's turn radius ŌĆö stays clear of the terrain rather than grazing it.

### `rim_inset_m` : f32

#2609: inward margin (metres) the on-station ring sampler holds off the
circular playable boundary (the terrain's `radius()`) for a domain that
clamps its ring to the circle (fixed-wing ŌĆö see
`DomainAdapter::ring_playable_radius`). A ring point within this margin of
the rim is treated as non-navigable, so the flown navigable **arc** ŌĆö with
the loop-follower's corner rounding and the airframe's turn radius ŌĆö stays
strictly inside the boundary rather than grazing it (the inward analog of
`bridge_clearance_margin_m`). Domains without a circle constraint
(surface/submarine) ignore it.

### `recenter_hysteresis_m` : f32

Re-center hysteresis (#2254), an absolute distance in metres: a
moving-anchor orbit (Screen escort, `FormingUp` rally) re-issues its
`Order::Orbit` only once the anchor has drifted more than
`recenter_hysteresis_m` from the active order's center. Below that, the
order is treated as already-active and is NOT re-issued ŌĆö so the
routing-cache and the #1968 plan-failed-warn throttle (both reset by
`set_immediate`) survive sub-threshold drift instead of churning every
tick. Kept well under the escort station-keeping tolerance so a
large-radius screen still tracks its protected entity closely. Mirrors the
`maintain_range` hysteresis (#2052).

### `station_keep_speed_mps` : f32

#2548: horizontal speed (m/s) below which a Screen leader treats its
protectee as stationary and station-keeps at a ring-offset point instead
of flying a continuous orbit (surface units only ŌĆö a fixed-wing stalls
and always orbits). This gates only the orbit ŌåÆ station-keep transition;
the reverse (station-keep ŌåÆ orbit) is position-based on
`recenter_hysteresis_m`, so the two directions give hysteresis for free.
Kept low so only a genuinely near-stopped protectee stops the screen; a
protectee under way orbits, and a slow creeper resumes orbiting via the
standoff-drift reverse gate.

## `StaleRetentionSection`

Retention policy for a `Lost` contact before it is purged from the
commander's contact picture (#2375). A contact is purged the first time
EITHER bound below is crossed, whichever comes first ŌĆö except a contact
under active battle-damage assessment (`BdaStatus::AwaitingBda`), which is
protected until its BDA deadline resolves regardless of these bounds
(#2402): purging it early would erase the track before the kill-
confirmation check ever runs.

Under the shipped `filter.process_noise_accel_mps2` (2.0 m/s┬▓), dead-
reckoned position Žā grows roughly quadratically with time since last
observation, so `max_position_sigma_m` is normally the bound that fires ŌĆö
around 4 minutes after a track goes `Lost` ŌĆö well before `max_age_secs`.
`max_age_secs` is a backstop for contacts whose position fix never
resolves numerically (e.g. a persistently unresolved fix) rather than the
common case.

### `max_age_secs` : f32

Hard ceiling (seconds since last observation) after which a `Lost`
contact is purged regardless of its position uncertainty.

### `max_position_sigma_m` : f32

Position 1Žā uncertainty (m) beyond which a `Lost` contact's dead-
reckoned fix is no longer tactically actionable and is purged early,
even under `max_age_secs`. Bearing-only (range-unresolved) contacts
have no scalar position Žā and are governed by `max_age_secs` alone.

### `recheck_interval_secs` : f32

How often a `Lost` contact that hasn't yet crossed either bound above
is re-checked (seconds). Bounds how far a purge can overshoot past the
instant a bound is actually crossed.

## `TrackFilterSection`

Tuning for the per-track recursive (Kalman) kinematic filter (DC-36.1).

### `process_noise_accel_mps2` : f32

Horizontal process-noise acceleration Žā (m/s┬▓) ŌĆö unmodelled
acceleration the constant-velocity model tolerates per predict step.
Larger values track manoeuvres faster but settle at a higher
covariance floor.

### `vertical_process_noise_accel_mps2` : f32

Vertical process-noise acceleration Žā (m/s┬▓) for the vertical
constant-velocity axis. Tuned separately from horizontal ŌĆö a missile's
vertical agility differs sharply from a ship's.

### `initial_position_var` : f32

Initial position-coordinate variance (m┬▓) when a track is first seeded.

### `initial_velocity_var` : f32

Initial velocity-coordinate variance ((m/s)┬▓) when a track is seeded.

### `initial_altitude_var` : f32

Initial vertical-position variance (m┬▓) when a track is seeded. Large ŌĆö
a fresh track's altitude is unknown until a height-finding sensor or a
resolved elevation-angle measurement constrains it.

### `initial_vertical_velocity_var` : f32

Initial vertical-velocity variance ((m/s)┬▓) when a track is seeded.

### `elevation_resolved_var_threshold` : f32

Vertical-position variance (m┬▓) at or below which `ElevationFix` flips to
`Resolved` (DC-1049). Above it, altitude is honestly Unknown.

## `MeasurementNoiseSection`

Tuning for how per-sensor measurement sigmas scale with detection SNR
(DC-36.1). Žā ŌłØ 1/ŌłÜSNR, so a detection `snr_reference_db` above the floor is
unscaled; weaker returns are noisier and stronger returns tighter, clamped.

### `snr_reference_db` : f32

SNR (dB) at which the authored per-sensor sigmas apply unscaled.

### `snr_scale_min` : f32

Lower bound on the SNR scale (strong detections never tighter than this).

### `snr_scale_max` : f32

Upper bound on the SNR scale (marginal detections never noisier).

### `min_sigma_m` : f32

Floor on any Žā (metres / m┬ĘsŌü╗┬╣) to keep the measurement covariance
positive-definite.

### `range_accuracy_k` : f32

Range-accuracy gain `k_R` in `Žā_R = dR/(k_R┬ĘŌłÜSNR)` (DC-1013). ŌłÜ2 textbook.

### `monopulse_k` : f32

Angular-accuracy gain `k` in `Žā_╬Ė = ╬Ė_bw/(k┬ĘŌłÜSNR)` (DC-1013). ~1.6 monopulse.

## `BearingOnlySection`

Tuning for bearing-only passive sensors (DC-36.2). A passive sensor measures
angle of arrival only; one bearing fixes direction but not range, so a fresh
bearing-only track is seeded along the measured bearing at an SNR-derived
best-guess range (DC-1061) with a huge down-range variance. The variance
stays large as a low-confidence prior; cross-fix / TMA / active observation
collapse it emergently through the filter.

### `initial_range_var` : f32

Down-range variance (m┬▓) of a bearing-only seed ŌĆö large enough that a single
bearing yields an effectively-unbounded *statistical* range (DC-1061: the
SNR band is a display/seed prior, not a fix, so the covariance stays large).

### `bearing_only_var_threshold` : f32

Major-axis position variance (m┬▓) above which range is unresolved and the
fix is `crate::PositionFix::RangeUnresolved`. A cross-fix / TMA / active
observation must collapse the down-range variance below this to resolve.

### `elevation_range_var_threshold` : f32

Major-axis horizontal position variance (m┬▓) at or below which a passive
elevation-angle measurement may be fused (DC-1051). Far tighter than
`bearing_only_var_threshold`: an elevation angle resolves altitude against
the estimated horizontal range, so the range must be tightly pinned before
the linearisation is stable. Above this the altitude stays honestly
Unknown (never diverges). This absolute gate is far tighter than the
range-unresolved classifier: a track can be `Resolved` yet still have a
range too loose for a stable elevation linearisation, so an absolute
threshold (not merely `!is_range_unresolved`) is the correct guard.

### `snr_near_range_m` : f32

Best-guess range (m) at or above the strong-SNR anchor (closest).

### `snr_far_range_m` : f32

Best-guess range (m) at or below the weak-SNR anchor (farthest / effective reach).

### `snr_strong_db` : f32

SNR (dB) at/above which the contact is treated as close (`snr_near_range_m`).

### `snr_weak_db` : f32

SNR (dB) at/below which the contact is treated as far (`snr_far_range_m`).

### `range_uncertainty_factor` : f32

Multiplicative band half-width (>1): `near = r_hat / k`, `far = r_hat * k`.

### `bearing_smoothing_tau_secs` : f32

Time constant (s) of the published-bearing EMA for range-unresolved
contacts (DC-1141). The raw per-scan measured bearing carries the full
angular noise (up to ~18┬░ 1Žā at weak SNR) ŌĆö the wire fix publishes a
smoothed direction instead, converging on a steady emitter while
tracking a crossing one with ~Žä of lag. The smoother folds only LIVE
measurements from the publishing sensor (never a seed position), so the
DC-1123 frozen-seed guarantee is preserved.

### `seeker_gh_dir_tau_secs` : f32

EMA time constant (s) for the DIRECTION output (`dir` / `los_dir`) of
a MISSILE's own-seeker LOS smoother (#1729). Distinct from
`bearing_smoothing_tau_secs` (5 s, used for the rendered team-bearing
line); the seeker must track the target direction with low lag during
the ~2ŌĆō3 s closure window so the PN law homes on a fresh LOS. A long Žä
here causes the missile to home on a stale direction and miss at fine
tick rates (100 ms).

### `seeker_gh_rate_tau_secs` : f32

Rate time constant (s) for a MISSILE's own-seeker LOS-rate g-h tracker
(#1729, #1822). The alpha-beta gains are DERIVED from `dt` and this Žä via
the critically-damped (Kalata) relations `╬Ė = exp(-dt/Žä)`, `g = 1-╬Ė┬▓`,
`h = (1-╬Ė)┬▓` inside `smooth_seeker` ŌĆö they are NOT fixed constants.
Constant gains are only valid at one `dt`: the velocity update
`rate += (h/dt)┬Ęresid` makes steady-state rate noise scale as `Žā/dt`, so a
fixed-gain tracker blows up at fast scan rates / fine ticks. Deriving the
gains from `dt` keeps the rate estimate's noise and bandwidth independent
of the scan interval (the engine must support any sensor scan rate and
variable `dt`). Larger Žä ŌåÆ smoother (lower-noise) but laggier rate.

## `TmaSection`

Tuning for the batch pseudo-linear TMA velocity solver (DC-1053). When a
passive-only track has two or more bearings in its time window, the solver
jointly estimates horizontal position+velocity `[px, pz, vx, vz]` by weighted
least-squares, gated on observability so velocity is reported only when the
geometry/time baseline actually constrains it.

### `window_secs` : f32

Length (s, sim time) of the per-track bearing history window. The
constant-velocity assumption must hold across it ŌĆö long enough for a
velocity baseline, short enough that a maneuver does not corrupt the fit.

### `max_measurements` : u32

Hard cap on retained measurements per track (bounded memory/compute);
the oldest are dropped first once exceeded.

### `velocity_resolved_var_threshold` : f32

Major-eigenvalue threshold ((m/s)┬▓) on the solved velocity covariance at
or below which velocity is reported `Resolved`. Above it, velocity is
honestly Unknown and the solver falls back to a position-only fix.

### `maneuver_residual_chi2` : f32

Reduced-Žć┬▓ (per degree of freedom) of the weighted batch residual above
which the target is judged to have maneuvered (broken the CV model); the
window is flushed so velocity re-converges on the new leg.

## `ClusteringSection`

Tuning for honest formation merging (DC-36.3). Closely-spaced targets a sensor
cannot resolve merge into one track; this knob shapes the merge/split
hysteresis.

### `split_hysteresis` : f32

Multiplier (> 1) applied to a sensor's resolution cell when deciding to
*split* an already-merged pair, versus the base cell used to *merge*. The
dead band between merge and split stops formations flickering (AC#4).

## `ClassificationSection`

Tuning for the staged contact classifier (DC-36.5). The classifier accumulates
a log-likelihood per candidate chassis from environment, kinematic-bucket,
size and acoustic evidence, then projects that distribution onto the NTDS tree
(symbol shape) and to a chassis-convergence verdict (symbol outline).

### `converge_threshold` : f32

Posterior probability a single chassis must hold for the classifier to
report it `crate::ChassisStatus::Converged` (and draw a solid outline).

### `ntds_project_threshold` : f32

Subtree probability mass an NTDS tree node must hold to be the displayed
symbol. Lower than `converge_threshold`, so the shape resolves to a class
before the outline goes solid.

### `max_cluster` : u32

Maximum size of an indistinguishable `crate::ChassisStatus::Cluster`
before the verdict degrades to `Open`.

### `ll_floor` : f32

Log-likelihood floor relative to the running maximum: no single
observation can push a candidate more than this far below the leader, so a
once-rejected chassis can recover ("absence is never asserted").

### `env_penalty` : f32

Per-observation log-likelihood penalty for a chassis whose environment
(air / surface / subsurface) does not match the observed elevation band.
Large, so the generic NTDS environment node resolves on the first look.
Applied only for a position-fixed contact (active sensor measures
elevation); a bearing-only contact contributes no environment evidence.

### `env_discrimination_margin` : f32

Probability-mass lead the top NTDS *environment* branch (air / surface /
subsurface / land) must hold over the runner-up before any node below the
root may be projected (#2765).

Subtree mass alone conflates "fits the evidence better" with "has more
chassis authored near this size": a height-blind or bearing-only look at a
10 m `usv_80` cannot separate it from a 10 m `mq_220`, yet air led
0.667/0.333 on chassis *count* and stamped a confident `Air` on a ship.
Both honest routes to an environment clear this by an order of magnitude ŌĆö
`env_penalty` crushes the rival branch to ~3e-4, and a decisive size (a
90 m hull has no air rival) pins the leader at ~1.0 ŌĆö so this only bites
the genuinely ambiguous case, where the honest answer is the root.

### `kin_weight` : f32

Per-observation log-likelihood bonus for a chassis whose kinematic bucket
matches the contact's *observed* kinematics. Soft, so physics-grounded
leaves (helicopter / fixed-wing / missile) resolve over several looks.

### `heli_max_speed_mps` : f32

Observed horizontal speed (m/s) at/below which an airborne contact reads
as a helicopter (hover-capable) rather than a fixed-wing.

### `missile_min_speed_mps` : f32

Observed horizontal speed (m/s) at/above which an airborne contact reads
as a missile rather than a fixed-wing.

### `torpedo_min_speed_mps` : f32

Observed horizontal speed (m/s) at/above which a subsurface contact reads
as a torpedo rather than a submarine.

### `speed_bucket_hysteresis` : f32

Schmitt-trigger dead-band factor (> 1) on the kinematic-bucket speed
thresholds (`heli_max` / `missile_min` / `torpedo_min`), #2480. A bucket is
*entered* at its bare threshold (prompt ŌĆö keeps `Unknown ŌåÆ Missile` upgrades
immediate, so the inbound-missile alarm still fires the instant a fast track
is classified) but only *left* once speed clears the threshold by this
factor (a minimum threshold exits at `threshold / factor`, a maximum at
`threshold * factor`). Without it, a track whose (noisy) speed straddles a
threshold has its credited bucket alternate every scan, so the projected
leaf flips (e.g. `Missile Ōåö Air`) and ŌĆö across the missile boundary ŌĆö
re-fires the auto-pause alarm on each re-entry. Same role as
`ClusteringSection::split_hysteresis` for the merge/split cell.

### `surface_max_speed_mps` : f32

Observed horizontal speed (m/s) above which a contact cannot be `Surface`
or `Subsurface` no matter what its (possibly still-converging, or
previously sea-level-pinned) altitude reads (#2386): kinematics dominate
altitude. 100.0 gives ~5.7x headroom over the fastest surface hull in the
game (USV-80, 17.4 m/s) ŌĆö comfortably clear of any legitimate surface or
submarine craft, same tuning scale as `heli_max_speed_mps`.

### `inbound_hostile_radius_m` : f32

Radius (m) around an own-team unit within which an inbound weapon's
closest point of approach flags it provisionally hostile (#2720). An
`Unknown` track that is missile-kinematic (`is_munition` class, or speed
at or above `missile_min_speed_mps`) *and* whose CPA against any own unit
falls within this radius while still closing is auto-classified `Enemy`
(provisionally - the IFF system overrules it). Sized to the doctrine
safety-range outer edge (`doctrine::DEFAULT_SAFETY_RANGE.outer` = 7500),
the outer boundary of a unit's threat-reaction envelope, so any inbound
weapon closing within the volume a screen guards is flagged.

### `size_sigma_frac` : f32

Fractional 1Žā of the size (length) likelihood, as a fraction of the
observed length. Smaller = size discriminates chassis more sharply.

### `min_size_sigma_m` : f32

Floor on the size Žā (metres), so a tiny contact still has finite width.

### `acoustic_sigma_log` : f32

1Žā of the acoustic likelihood, in log10(sonar-noise) units.

### `signature_bucket_penalty` : f32

Per-look penalty for a candidate whose physics bucket disagrees with a
signature read (DC-36.6). Large (env-scale) so a capable read overrides
the speed-derived bucket ŌĆö a fast helicopter still resolves rotary.

### `signature_match_weight` : f32

Per-look bonus (matched) / penalty (mismatched) for a candidate whose
propulsion class agrees with a signature read. Narrows same-bucket hulls.

## `FireControlSection`

Fire-control lock tuning (DC-946.1). The acquisition dwell a cued
track-capable sensor must hold before `Acquiring ŌåÆ Locked` is a **per-sensor**
characteristic (`SensorDef::acquire_dwell_secs`) ŌĆö a search/FCS radar confirms
a new track over ~1 s, while a fired weapon's terminal seeker is slaved to the
launch solution and acquires near-instantly (#2201). A lock that loses its
target ages out (the designation is released) `lock_drop_after_secs` after
going `Lost`.

### `lock_drop_after_secs` : f32

Seconds after a lock goes `Lost` (eval fails / gate no longer fits) at
which the designation is released and the track de-designated.
